axiom ImproveCredit : obj → Prop

axiom QualifyMortgage : obj → Prop

axiom S_to_A : ∀ x : obj, SaveAgg x → AffordDownPayment x
-- If someone improves their credit score, they can qualify for a mortgage.
axiom I_to_Q : ∀ x : obj, ImproveCredit x → QualifyMortgage x
-- The person either saves aggressively or improves their credit.
axiom Disj : SaveAgg Person ∨ ImproveCredit Person
-- If someone qualifies for a mortgage, they can buy their dream home.
axiom Q_to_B : ∀ x : obj, QualifyMortgage x → BuyDreamHome x

-- Given: they could not afford a down payment.
axiom NotAfford : ¬AffordDownPayment Person

-- From the premises and NotAfford, we can actually prove they buy their dream home.
theorem buy_dream_home : BuyDreamHome Person := by
  have notSave : ¬SaveAgg Person := by
    intro hs
    have ha : AffordDownPayment Person := S_to_A Person hs
    exact NotAfford ha
  have hi : ImproveCredit Person := by
    cases Disj with
    | inl hs => exact False.elim (notSave hs)
    | inr hi => exact hi
  have hq : QualifyMortgage Person := I_to_Q Person hi
  exact Q_to_B Person hq

-- Cannot prove ¬BuyDreamHome Person from given axioms; in fact, we proved BuyDreamHome Person above.
</lean>

-- Predicates
axiom Service : obj → Prop          -- offers great customer service
axiom Reputation : obj → Prop       -- reputation grows

axiom RevenueImproves : obj → Prop  -- revenue will improve

axiom P2 : Events Store → MoreCustomers Store
axiom P3 : Service Store ∨ Events Store
axiom P4 : MoreCustomers Store → RevenueImproves Store

-- Given: service quality decreased (i.e., not excellent service)

-- From the given premises, we can actually prove that revenue improves.
theorem revenue_improves : RevenueImproves Store := by
  -- From Service ∨ Events and ¬Service, deduce Events
  have hEvents : Events Store := by
    cases P3 with
    | inl hService => exact False.elim (DecreasedService hService)
    | inr hEvents  => exact hEvents
  -- Then MoreCustomers and hence RevenueImproves
  have hMore : MoreCustomers Store := P2 hEvents
  exact P4 hMore

-- Since RevenueImproves Store is provable, we cannot prove its negation ¬RevenueImproves Store.
</lean>

axiom RedoLivingRoom : obj → Prop
axiom ReadBlogs : obj → Prop
axiom Ideas : obj → Prop

axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, WatchedDIY x → Skills x
-- If someone has decorating skills, they redo their living room.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Skills x → RedoLivingRoom x

-- Matt either watched DIY videos or read interior design blogs (or both).
axiom Disj : WatchedDIY Matt ∨ ReadBlogs Matt
-- If someone reads interior design blogs, then they get new decorating ideas.

  intro hS
  have hR : RedoLivingRoom Matt := R2 Matt hS
  exact T_not_redo hR

  intro hW
  apply not_skills_matt
  exact R1 Matt hW

theorem read_matt : ReadBlogs Matt := by
  have h := Disj
  have hnw := not_watched_matt
  apply Or.elim h
  · intro hw
    exact False.elim (hnw hw)
  · intro hr
    exact hr

theorem ideas_matt : Ideas Matt := by
  exact R3 Matt read_matt

-- From the premises we can derive Ideas Matt, so we cannot prove ¬Ideas Matt.

axiom Person : Type
axiom Mary : Person

axiom Sale : Prop
axiom PricesUp : Prop
axiom Payday : Prop

axiom Stock : Person → Prop
axiom List : Person → Prop
axiom PlanBuy : Person → Prop
axiom PlanAhead : Person → Prop

-- If the supermarket has a sale, then people will stock up on groceries.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, Sale → Stock x
-- If someone makes a shopping list, then they plan what to buy.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, List x → PlanBuy x
-- Either the store has a sale, or people don't plan what to buy, or there's a sale but they don't plan ahead.
axiom R3 : Sale ∨ (∀ x : Person, ¬PlanBuy x) ∨ (Sale ∧ (∀ x : Person, ¬PlanAhead x))
-- If payday was this week, then people make a shopping list.
axiom R4 : Payday → ∀ x : Person, List x
-- Either it was payday this week, or prices went up recently, or both.
axiom R5 : Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp)

-- From "Mary did not stock up on groceries" we can derive that prices went up recently.
theorem prices_up_if_mary_not_stock : (¬Stock Mary) → PricesUp := by
  intro hns
  -- ¬Sale
  have hNotSale : ¬Sale := by
    intro hSale
    have hSm : Stock Mary := R1 Mary hSale
    exact hns hSm
  -- From R3 and ¬Sale, deduce ∀x, ¬PlanBuy x
  have hNoPlan : ∀ x : Person, ¬PlanBuy x := by
    have h := R3
    cases h with
    | inl hSale =>
      have hf : False := hNotSale hSale
      intro x
      exact False.elim hf
    | inr hRest =>
      cases hRest with
      | inl hNoPlan' =>
        exact hNoPlan'
      | inr hSaleAnd =>
        have hSale := hSaleAnd.left
        have hf : False := hNotSale hSale
        intro x
        exact False.elim hf
  -- From hNoPlan and R4, deduce ¬Payday
  have hNotPay : ¬Payday := by
    intro hPay
    have hListAll : ∀ x : Person, List x := R4 hPay
    have hPBMary : PlanBuy Mary := R2 Mary (hListAll Mary)
    have hNPBMary : ¬PlanBuy Mary := hNoPlan Mary
    exact hNPBMary hPBMary
  -- From R5 and ¬Payday, deduce PricesUp
  have hPU : PricesUp := by
    have h := R5
    cases h with
    | inl hPay =>
      exact False.elim (hNotPay hPay)
    | inr hRest =>
      cases hRest with
      | inl hP =>
        exact hP
      | inr hBoth =>
        exact hBoth.right
  exact hPU

-- Cannot prove ¬PricesUp from given axioms (in fact, we proved PricesUp from ¬Stock Mary)
</lean>

  cases D2 with

axiom Tastes : obj → Prop
axiom PrepareSalad : obj → Prop

axiom ThickensPerfectly : Prop

-- If someone adds salt, the stew becomes savory
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, AddsSalt x → Savory
-- If someone stirs continuously, the stew thickens perfectly
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, StirsContinuously x → ThickensPerfectly
-- Either someone adds salt or the stew does not thicken perfectly (inclusive or)
axiom Law1 : (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) ∨ ¬ThickensPerfectly
-- If someone tastes the stew, they stir it continuously
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Tastes x → StirsContinuously x
-- Either someone tastes the stew or someone prepares a salad (inclusive or)

-- From ¬Savory, we can actually derive that someone prepares a salad
theorem salad_exists_from_not_savory : ¬Savory → ∃ x : obj, PrepareSalad x := by
  intro notSavory
  -- No one adds salt
  have noSalt : ¬(∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) := by
    intro h
    cases h with
    | intro a ha =>
      have hs : Savory := R1 a ha
      exact notSavory hs
  -- Therefore the stew does not thicken perfectly

      exact notThick

    intro h
    cases h with
    | intro a ha =>

    intro h
    cases h with
